meaning: vi.食言,违例出牌vt.否认,拒绝n.出牌违例
examples: Any more colourful candidate could renege on a deal.
It says firmly that the EU must not renege on its promise.
For one thing history tells us that the Republican Party would renege on its side of any deal as soon as it got the chance.
Anil took Mukesh to court for allegedly trying to renege on the gas supply terms.
"Defendants now renege on those promises and press an act of war defense, " the lawyers wrote.
